weak_aliases.reason: Shared generic alias not sufficient to indicate same committee; must be project-scoped.
Generic base alias and project-specific alias; the project-specific one is more precise.
Generic aliases that could apply to any CAC.
Long alias, but project-specific, not generic.

suggested_rules: For project-specific committees, the project scope (corridor, route, service area) is a strong identity field. Records with different scopes never merge even if the base committee name is identical.
suggestion.rationale: All records are community advisory committees for different bus projects. Contradiction test (test 1) applies: description/project scope differs across Flushing-Jamaica, Webster, and Woodhaven/Cross Bay. The two Woodhaven/Cross Bay records (entity_community-advisory-committee-2015 and entity_woodhaven-cac-2014) differ only by year suffix and represent the same durable committee; they merge under the lifecycle record rule (test 2). All cross-project pairs are do_not_merge. The high confidence is due to clear project attribution in payload identity fields.
